conclusions.statement: Score translation accuracy with multi-reference semantic cosine as a SCREEN, not a verdict — reserve a blind LLM/SME judge for the ambiguous tail.
conclusions.detail: Single-reference string/cosine breaks on valid Korean synonyms; multi-ref (all KR synonyms) chrF + max BGE cosine fixes that. But even multi-ref understates valid-but-divergent translations: it rated organism context +2.8 chrF while a blind judge preferred it 69% vs 31%.
conclusions.author: research-agent

conclusions.statement: Back-translation LINKS Korean terms to the right SNOMED concept ~63-68%@1 at full scale, but is a poor ERROR detector — the round-trip LLM normalises errors away.
conclusions.detail: Full 36k extension: recall@1 63% (hybrid) / 68% (dense). As an MT error detector AUC ~0.5: recovery is identical for correct and incorrect candidates.
conclusions.author: research-agent

conclusions.statement: A non-generative direct cross-lingual check beats back-translation for error detection (AUC 0.58-0.62 vs 0.51), but only a Korean reference gives a strong (0.98) check.
conclusions.detail: Skipping the laundering LLM recovers error signal; absolute strength is still weak reference-free (BGE compresses cross-lingual cosine). Strong detection needs a Korean reference -> multi-reference semantic validation where references exist.
conclusions.author: research-agent

conclusions.statement: SNOMED parent context improves EN->KO translation on the AMBIGUOUS tail (judge ~2:1 on organisms / qualifier values) but hurts concrete types — add it conditionally, fenced.
conclusions.detail: Neutral on the mean (harsh metric), but a blind judge shows organism 69% / qualifier 66% wins and body-structure/disorder losses. Naive '(tag)' formatting leaks into output; fence the context.
conclusions.author: research-agent
